21.correct: Carbon is incorporated into living organisms through photosynthesis, in which plants fix atmospheric carbon dioxide into organic compounds (glucose).

wrong.A: Respiration releases carbon dioxide rather than fixing it into organisms.
wrong.C: Fermentation is a form of respiration that releases carbon dioxide.
wrong.D: Decomposition returns carbon to the environment, it does not incorporate it into living bodies.

21.fact: Photosynthesis is the entry point of carbon into the food chain.

22.correct: The Kalbeliya dance of Rajasthan is inspired by the movements of the snake, reflecting its origin among the snake-charmer community.

wrong.A: It is not based on the peacock.
wrong.C: It does not imitate a horse.
wrong.D: It does not imitate an elephant.

22.fact: Kalbeliya is recognised on UNESCO's Intangible Cultural Heritage list.

23.correct: The Ganga is considered especially holy for the ritual bath on Mauni Amavasya, when huge numbers of devotees bathe (notably at Prayagraj).

wrong.A: The Yamuna is sacred but the Ganga is the principal river of Mauni Amavasya bathing.
wrong.C: The Godavari is holy but not the focus of Mauni Amavasya.
wrong.D: The Saraswati is a mythical/underground river at the Sangam, not the bathing river itself.

23.fact: Mauni Amavasya is one of the most important bathing days of the Magh Mela / Kumbh.

24.correct: Zeliang and Li are folk songs/dances of Nagaland (associated with Naga tribal traditions).

wrong.A: Sikkim has its own distinct folk traditions.
wrong.C: Arunachal Pradesh has different tribal dances.

24.fact: Nagaland's Hornbill Festival showcases the folk music and dance of its many tribes.

25.correct: A 'light year' is defined as the distance that light travels in one year - it is a unit of distance, not time.

wrong.A: It is not the distance covered in one hour.
wrong.C: 'Speed x time' is a general formula, not the precise definition of a light year.
wrong.D: The time for one revolution of the Earth is a year, not a light year.

25.fact: One light year is about 9.46 trillion kilometres.

26.correct: A radio telescope is used to observe radio waves coming from space, allowing study of objects invisible to optical telescopes.

wrong.A: An optical telescope collects visible light.
wrong.B: A reflecting telescope is a type of optical telescope.
wrong.D: A refracting telescope is also an optical telescope using lenses.

26.fact: India's Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT) near Pune is one of the world's largest radio telescope arrays.

27.correct: A standard One Day International (ODI) cricket match consists of two innings, each of 50 overs, with a break in between.

wrong.B: It is not a single 50-over innings; both teams bat.
wrong.C: Two innings of 20 overs each describes a T20 match.
wrong.D: A single 20-over innings is not the ODI format.

27.fact: ODIs were introduced in 1971; the format was originally 60 overs a side before settling at 50.

28.correct: India hosted the fourth BRICS Summit in 2012 (in New Delhi).

wrong.A: Brazil hosted other BRICS summits, not the fourth.
wrong.D: South Africa hosted the fifth summit (2013).

28.fact: The New Delhi summit's theme focused on stability, security and prosperity.

29.correct: Rainwater harvesting mainly helps in groundwater recharge by capturing rain and allowing it to percolate into the ground.

wrong.A: It does not directly reduce air pollution.
wrong.B: It helps reduce soil erosion, it does not cause it.
wrong.D: It does not increase global temperature.

29.fact: Rainwater harvesting also reduces surface runoff and urban flooding.

30.correct: Bhim Singh Bhavesh was awarded the Padma Shri in 2025 for his work for the Musahar community.

wrong.A: Sunita Williams is an astronaut, not the awardee for this work.
wrong.C: Nirmala Devi is not the recipient named here.
wrong.D: Arundhati Bhattacharya is a banker, not the awardee for Musahar welfare.

30.fact: The Musahar are among the most marginalised communities, mainly in Bihar and eastern Uttar Pradesh.

31.correct: Per the answer key, Harikrishnan A Ra became India's 87th chess Grandmaster in 2025.

wrong.A: Gukesh became a GM much earlier and is the World Champion, not the 87th GM.
wrong.C: Parag Jain is not identified as the 87th GM here.
wrong.D: D. Gukesh is the same player as 'Gukesh', not the 87th GM.

31.fact: India has produced a rapidly growing number of chess Grandmasters, led by Viswanathan Anand's legacy.

32.correct: Kaziranga National Park (Assam) is famous for the one-horned rhinoceros, which it protects in large numbers.

wrong.A: The Bengal tiger is found there too, but Kaziranga is best known for the rhino.
wrong.B: Elephants are present but not its defining species.
wrong.C: The Asiatic lion is found in Gir (Gujarat), not Kaziranga.

32.fact: Kaziranga is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and hosts about two-thirds of the world's one-horned rhinos.

33.correct: The Bhakra Dam is built on the Sutlej river (part of the Bhakra-Nangal project).

wrong.B: Nagarjuna Sagar is on the Krishna river.
wrong.C: The Hirakud Dam is on the Mahanadi river.
wrong.D: The Tehri Dam is on the Bhagirathi river.

33.fact: The Bhakra Dam is one of the highest gravity dams in India, key to irrigation and power in the north-west.

34.correct: Two long parallel wires carrying current in the same direction attract each other, due to the interaction of their magnetic fields.

wrong.A: They attract, not repel, when currents flow in the same direction.
wrong.C: They are not unaffected; there is a definite force.
wrong.D: They do not oscillate under this steady force.

34.fact: Currents in opposite directions repel each other; this effect is used to define the ampere.

35.correct: The PM Dhan-Dhaanya Krishi Yojana (launched 2025), with an annual outlay of about Rs 24,000 crore, targets 100 low-performing agricultural districts over 6 years.

wrong.A: 3 years is not the scheme's duration.
wrong.D: 10 years is longer than the announced period.

35.fact: The scheme aims to raise productivity and incomes in agriculturally backward districts.

36.correct: Under the Mughal mansabdari system, a mansabdar's military responsibility was to maintain a fixed number of horsemen (sawars) ready for the emperor's service.

wrong.B: Collecting taxes was the job of revenue officials, not the core military duty.
wrong.C: Administering justice was a separate function.
wrong.D: Overseeing land distribution was not the mansabdar's defined military duty.

36.fact: A mansabdar's rank had two numbers - 'zat' (personal status) and 'sawar' (cavalry to be maintained).

37.correct: In December 2024 the Ministry of Tourism launched the 'Paryatan Mitra and Paryatan Didi' initiative to promote responsible tourism by training local guides/hosts.

wrong.B: 'Tourism in Bharat' is not the name of this initiative.
wrong.C: 'Dekho Apna Desh' is a separate domestic-tourism campaign.
wrong.D: 'Incredible India 2.0' is a marketing campaign, not this initiative.

37.fact: The initiative trains locals as friendly tourism ambassadors at key destinations.

38.correct: Project Akashteer is an automated air-defence control and reporting system for the Indian Army, integrating radars and sensors to manage the airspace.

wrong.A: It is not a drone attacker.
wrong.C: It is not a laser shield system.
wrong.D: It is not an AI-based rocket launcher.

38.fact: Akashteer enables coordinated, automated control of low-level airspace and air-defence weapons.

39.correct: In April 2025, the Raman Research Institute developed an affordable electro-fluidic device for the early screening of Sickle Cell Disease (SCD).

wrong.A: Tata Memorial Centre focuses on cancer care, not this device.
wrong.C: CSIR is a broad research body, but the device was credited to the Raman Research Institute.
wrong.D: AIIMS is a medical institute but not the developer cited here.

39.fact: India runs a National Sickle Cell Anaemia Elimination Mission targeting tribal and vulnerable populations.

40.correct: Both the forehand (I) and the backhand (II) are standard groundstrokes played in a tennis match, so 'Both I and II' is correct.

wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since both are common tennis shots.

40.fact: Other tennis strokes include the serve, volley, smash and drop shot.
